prevalence.population: Brazilian adults aged 45 years and older
prevalence.source: Brazilian Society of Cardiology (SBC) guidelines and VIGITEL survey data
prevalence.trend: increasing due to aging population and rising cardiovascular risk factors

incidence.population: Brazilian population aged 40 years and older
incidence.source: DataSUS and published epidemiological studies in Revista Brasileira de Cardiologia

burden.source: Global Burden of Disease (GBD) Study 2019 extrapolated to 2024 and DataSUS mortality records

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Based on clinical symptoms (dyspnea, fatigue), echocardiographic evidence of reduced ejection fraction (<40%) or preserved ejection fraction with structural heart disease, and NYHA functional classification, as per SBC guidelines

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Limited routine screening; opportunistic via SUS primary care for high-risk groups, covering approximately 40% of adults over 40
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Approximately 1.8 million registered in SUS system
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Around 35% of patients achieve effective management with guideline-directed therapy in SUS

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Brazilian Society of Cardiology (SBC) diagnostic standards, emphasizing echocardiography and biomarkers like NT-proBNP
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: SUS protocols follow SBC guidelines with availability of ACE inhibitors, beta-blockers, diuretics, and SGLT2 inhibitors; device therapy limited in rural areas
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High burden from Chagas disease in endemic areas, socioeconomic inequalities in access to specialized care, urban-rural disparities, and overburdened SUS in underserved regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: SUS provides universal access to essential medications and multidisciplinary care through family health strategy units, with national heart failure registries for monitoring
